trans-unit.source: Manage AI providers, models, and MCP tools.
AI Universe — master extension for AI providers, governance, MCP server, and dynamic tools in TYPO3.
Your backend user group does not have permission to open this AI Universe tab.
Guided setup wizard for TYPO3 backend usergroup AI permissions.
Only TYPO3 administrators can configure AI Permissions.
Build AI-powered TYPO3 extensions on top of AI Foundation — a shared gateway built on symfony/ai-platform.
Every extension talks to one hub — AI Foundation routes to providers and MCP.
Architecture diagram: extensions connect through AI Foundation to AI providers and MCP clients
six shipped extensions plug in the exact same way

T3AF (AI Foundation for TYPO3) turns your TYPO3 installation into a fully AI-ready platform. Complete AI-infrastructure for whole TYPO3 team, zero setup. Manage every AI provider, MCP servers & tools, brand context, prompts, users permissions, and budget in one native backend module. You stay in control, your every TYPO3 team (editors, integrators, developers & administrator) works with AI on one governed, self-hosted foundation. Use it standalone — or as the foundation that powers the complete AI Universe.
Built on symfony/ai-platform — OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Mistral, Ollama and more work through the same call. Swap providers in settings, not code.
Token-based billing, streaming, and pre-submit estimates come free — no per-feature cost tables to maintain.
Your feature cards, prompts, and settings drawers render directly inside AI Foundation. No custom module chrome.
Expose tools to Cursor, Claude Desktop, and AI agents with a single attribute — no server to stand up.
Your code never talks to a provider directly — AI Foundation sits in between.
Three steps from a fresh extension to your first AI call.
Add EXT:ns_t3af as a dependency of your extension. No API keys or SDKs to install — providers are configured once, centrally.
Resolve it through TYPO3's dependency injection or GeneralUtility::makeInstance(). It's the only class you need to know.
Pass an extensionKey and featureKey — AI Foundation resolves the provider via symfony/ai-platform, tracks credits, and logs the request for you.
Inject AiServiceInterface, call complete() — routing, providers, and billing are handled for you.

Save as Classes/Service/MyFeatureService.php. Declare EXT:ns_t3af in ext_emconf.php, autowire your extension in Configuration/Services.yaml, then replace Vendor\MyExtension and my_extension with your values.

this is the same call T3AI uses for its SEO meta description generator — extensionKey: %s, featureKey: %s. Swap the prompt and feature key for your own extension and you inherit the same provider routing, credits, and logging.
Six extension points — pick the ones your extension needs.
Connect via AiServiceInterface — request flow, AiOptions, and a minimal working example.
Ship proprietary, on-prem, or niche cloud adapters alongside the built-in symfony/ai-platform bridges.
Register prompt catalog providers that show up under AI Foundation → AI Prompts.
Add feature cards and per-site settings schemas to AI Foundation → AI Features.
Give editors a per-feature default AI provider dropdown for SEO, pages, content, translation.
Register tools from your extension for Cursor, Claude Desktop, and the backend MCP playground.
A growing set of features already running on the same gateway — click through to each extension's docs.
Install EXT:ns_t3af and your child extension inherits every capability below.
EXT:extension_key depends on EXT:ns_t3af — no backend module, settings screen, or logging table to build yourself.
Your extension's activity, credits, and health surface here automatically — no widget to build.
Every provider an admin connects is instantly usable by your extension.
Brand voice, tone, and site context are injected into your calls automatically.
Register a tool once and it's exposed to every connected MCP client.
Your feature card renders inside AI Foundation → AI Features with per-site settings.
Every call is metered, credited, and logged centrally.
Roles are enforced by AI Foundation — your extension inherits the same guardrails.
Ship a prompt catalog and it appears under AI Foundation → AI Prompts.
AI Foundation runs one shared MCP server for the whole TYPO3 install. Register a tool from your extension and it's callable by Cursor, Claude Desktop, and any MCP client.
Hosted server URL + OAuth 2.1 — recommended for Claude Desktop, Cursor, and cloud agents.
Persistent bidirectional connection for low-latency, streaming-heavy clients.
Run the MCP server in a container alongside your TYPO3 stack.
Tunnel stdio transport over SSH for locked-down environments.
Zero-install bridge — launch a local MCP proxy with a single npx command.
A thin local process bridges stdio clients to the remote HTTP server.
Direct process-to-process transport — ideal for local development.
Register a tool from your extension
Tag a service mcp.tool, annotate execute() with #[McpTool], flush caches.
Copy the server URL and token from MCP Server → Connect, or launch the local bridge with npx.
Installation, architecture, MCP server setup, and every extension point in one place.
EXT:ns_t3af_extended on GitHub is a reference extension with every integration hook — providers, prompts, features, MCP tools, access, and AiServiceInterface usage.
Dashboard for your AI Foundation — requests, usage, and system health.
No MCP tool calls were logged in this period yet.
No active MCP clients are connected right now.
Configured AI provider connections. Add your own API keys per provider.
Manage server status, connection modes, and client access tokens.
No draft workspace exists yet. MCP changes should be staged in a workspace before publishing to Live.
Ask an administrator or a user with Workspaces module access to create a workspace.
Could not create workspace. Please try again or create one manually in the Workspaces module.
Choose your preferred method to connect MCP clients to this TYPO3 instance.
Connect over the internet using OAuth authentication (recommended).
For MCP clients that need a local bridge via mcp-remote.
Run the MCP server locally via TYPO3 CLI (stdio).
Connect MCP clients to this TYPO3 instance over the internet using OAuth authentication. This is the recommended method for most use cases.
No %1$s token found. Create an %1$s token to connect %1$s workflows to this TYPO3 instance.
URL tokens are sensitive — anyone with the link can access TYPO3 as your backend user. Use OAuth when possible.
For MCP clients that don't support remote connections natively. The mcp-remote package acts as a local bridge, connecting to this TYPO3 instance on behalf of your MCP client.
Your existing OAuth token will be included in the URL as a query parameter.
No mcp-remote token found. Create one to generate a token URL for local MCP clients.

The token URL includes your authentication token in the URL. Only use this method when your MCP client doesn't support Authorization headers or when using mcp-remote configurations that require tokens in the URL.
For local development where your MCP client has direct shell access to this TYPO3 installation.
Local only: Only works when your client has shell access to TYPO3
Long-running process: The CLI server runs continuously and may accumulate memory usage over time. Restart periodically if you notice performance issues.
Code changes: Changes to PHP code won't be reflected immediately. You'll need to restart the CLI server to pick up code modifications.
Manage your active OAuth tokens. Each token represents an authorized MCP client connection.
Manage all active OAuth tokens across backend users. Each token represents an authorized MCP client connection.
Pick your MCP client and follow the setup steps.
Secure Internet access with OAuth 2.0 authentication.
Development-only stdio bridge via vendor/bin/typo3 mcp:server.
URL-embedded token bridge for clients without Bearer header support.
Real-time WebSocket transport via reverse proxy.
Configure your reverse proxy to upgrade HTTP connections to WebSocket on the MCP path. Clients connect with WSS and send the same OAuth Bearer token.
Streamable HTTP at %s remains the primary transport. Enable WSS only when your proxy supports Upgrade headers and you need persistent connections.
Run the MCP stdio server inside your web container.
Use this when your MCP client runs on the host and talks to TYPO3 through Docker or DDEV.
Runs the MCP stdio server inside your web container via docker exec.
For DDEV, replace the container name with your project's web container.
Secure remote access through an SSH port forward.
Forward a local port to the TYPO3 HTTPS endpoint, then point MCP clients at https://localhost:8787/mcp.
After the tunnel is up, use the Remote (OAuth) server URL pattern with localhost, or %s directly if you forward to the MCP path.
Bridge HTTP MCP through the mcp-remote npm package.
For desktop clients that only support stdio, run mcp-remote as a local subprocess.
Issue and revoke long-lived OAuth bearer tokens for integrations.
No active tokens for your account.
Require clients to present a signed certificate. The connection itself becomes the credential — no tokens needed for verified clients.
mTLS is recommended for financial services, government, and on-premise TYPO3 deployments where token leakage must be completely eliminated.
Upload your Certificate Authority certificate. Only clients whose certs are signed by this CA will be accepted.
Client certificate validation (mTLS) is planned for a future release. Use OAuth Bearer tokens and the IP allowlist until then.
CA certificate (PEM) — available in a future release
No data for this period yet.
Install an MCP-compatible VS Code extension.
Use OAuth or bearer token auth.
Install JetBrains AI Assistant MCP plugin.
Create a Raycast Script Command or Extension.
In ChatGPT developer settings, add a custom MCP connector.
One redirect URI per line. Used for OAuth authorization code flow with MCP clients.
Space-separated scopes granted to new OAuth tokens (e.g. mcp:read mcp:write mcp:tools).
Go to Settings → Integrations in Claude Desktop.
Enter an integration name (for example: New TYPO3 site).
Claude Desktop will automatically handle OAuth authentication.
In your n8n workflow, add an MCP Client node.
Set Server Transport to HTTP Streamable.
Paste the bearer token from above.
In manus, add a new MCP server connection.
Set Server Name to your TYPO3 site name.
Add a custom Authorization header: Bearer <token>
Debug tools, resources, and prompts with MCP Inspector:
Run the command above in your terminal (requires Node.js).
The Inspector UI opens in your browser with parameters pre-configured.
Browse and test TYPO3 MCP tools.
Open your MCP client settings and add a new remote server.
Use HTTP Streamable transport when available.
For OAuth-capable clients, complete the authorization flow when prompted.
For Bearer-only clients, create a token under the n8n or manus tabs above.
AI tools exposed via Model Context Protocol — power your AI extensions and enable custom developer integrations.
AI Skills + MCP Tools — linked by extension
Each AI extension ships with a set of MCP tools and a downloadable .md AI Skill file for your assistant. Type the skill trigger in your chat to activate the workflow.
Custom MCP Tools for Other Extensions

Enable your own TYPO3 extension tables as MCP tools. Scan installed extensions with Discover Extension Tables, or register tables via Code Configuration in ext_localconf.php. Each enabled table automatically generates 9 CRUD tools.
No AI Universe extensions registered yet. Built-in core tools appear when no extension catalog is configured.
Enable this table to activate the 9 auto-generated MCP tools.
Server-defined prompts the AI client can invoke by name via prompts/list and prompts/get. Served dynamically — no download required.
Prompt templates complement your skill files. Skill files require manual download and setup per client. MCP Prompts are served automatically to any connected client that calls prompts/list.
Unique snake_case identifier exposed via prompts/get (e.g. weekly_content_review).
Use {{argumentName}} placeholders matching the arguments JSON below.
Tools created via Create Custom Tool. Register PHP handlers in your extension or point to REST/webhook endpoints.
No custom tools yet. Use Create Custom Tool in the sidebar to add one.
Built-in tools shipped with AI Universe for page reads, content listing, TCA schema, workspaces, files, and generic record writes via DataHandler.
No TYPO3 Core MCP tools registered.
Configure external MCP servers and monitor connector health.
Control extension-level AI capabilities, defaults, and access.
Inspect request logs, usage analytics, and export usage data.
System log entries across all AI extensions — sync, training, errors and scheduler events.
Retention is managed via Scheduler & CLI (t3af:ai-logs:cleanup --days, default 90 days).
No log entries found for the selected filters.
Manage reusable prompt templates for all AI modules.
Run AI commands manually or automate them with TYPO3 scheduler tasks.
Tab shell is ready. Integrate feature-specific UI and data sources here.
Enable ext-sodium on the PHP used by the webserver, then restart PHP-FPM/Apache. API keys cannot be stored securely without it.
TYPO3 Environment → PHP Info → search for "sodium". Or ask your host to enable ext-sodium and restart PHP-FPM.
Set SYS/encryptionKey in the Install Tool (Configuration). Credentials cannot be encrypted without it.
Enable ext-curl on the PHP used by the webserver. Outbound AI and credits API calls need it.
Add at least one AI provider under AI Providers.
Set a default provider so extensions know which connection to prefer.
Each enabled provider has a stored credential reference.
One or more providers are missing API credentials.
Optional — enable and verify when your MCP integration is connected.
%1$s requests logged in the selected window.
No requests recorded yet — trigger a test call from an AI-powered extension.
Add providers to start logging AI traffic.
No failing AI Universe scheduler tasks detected.
%1$s scheduler task(s) reported a failure — inspect Scheduler & CLI.
No AI Universe scheduler tasks found yet.
Prompt library UI is upcoming — defaults apply until custom prompts ship.
T3Planet license and API token are connected.
Activate T3Planet Credits to load balance and pricing.
Balance and bundles are synced from T3Planet.
Activate credits or refresh the dashboard to load your balance.
%1$s T3Planet credit requests in the selected period.
No T3Planet credit traffic in this period yet.
MCP server is reachable and responding.
MCP server is offline or not configured — check MCP Server tab.
No default profile — set up brand voice in AI Context
%1$s · %2$s%% complete — finish profile in AI Context
A UserTSconfig budget cap is configured for AI spend.
No daily cost cap detected — set nst3af.budget.maxCost in UserTSconfig.
No custom prompts yet — add prompts under AI Prompts.
%1$s prompts in library — review custom entries under AI Prompts.
You've installed the AI Foundation extension. This quick wizard gets your suite ready in under 2 minutes.
You can skip any step and configure it later from the respective module tabs.
T3Planet Credits or your own OpenAI, Anthropic, Google keys
Enable SEO, Content, Translation, Media and more
Enable tool-use and external system integrations
Choose how your TYPO3 AI extensions access language models. You can change this at any time from the AI Providers tab.
Use T3Planet's hosted AI infrastructure. No API keys, predictable billing, all features work out of the box.
Bundles from €6 · 250 to 8,000 credits, never expire · gpt-4o, Claude 3.5, Gemini Pro available
Connect OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible provider directly. You manage billing with each provider.
Choose the AI provider that will power your extensions. You can add more providers later from the AI Providers tab.
When T3Planet Credits is available, keys stay off your server.
You chose hosted capacity. No provider row is required — finish the wizard and enable credits when your account is ready.
T3Planet's infrastructure will handle all AI requests. No provider configuration needed.
All good — T3Planet will handle model routing automatically. Click Continue to activate your AI extensions.
Enter your %1$s API key. It will be stored encrypted in TYPO3's configuration.
Configure your %1$s connection. No API key is required for local Ollama.
Run Ollama locally — no cloud API key required
This field is disabled here — use AI Providers → Edit provider to store secrets.
Secrets are encrypted when saved on the provider record.
T3Planet manages all authentication securely. Your AI requests are routed through T3Planet's infrastructure.
No vendor lock-in — switch any time
Select which AI extensions to activate. You can change these at any time from the AI Features tab.
Configure detailed settings later under AI Features.
Meta tags, page titles, focus keywords
Generate full page layouts from prompts
Extension-specific modules continue to register themselves automatically.
Install the AI Content extension (ns_t3ai) to manage SEO, Content, Translation and Media feature toggles here.
Set a brand foundation so AI output matches your voice. Brand name, industry, and tone tags are required; you can refine the full profile later in AI Context.
Write like a trusted expert — clear and direct. Not like a salesperson — no jargon or hype.
Voice description is optional here. Add personas, rules, and more in the AI Context module after setup.
The MCP (Model Context Protocol) server allows your LLM to call external tools and APIs — Slack, Notion, databases, and more.
Full connector setup is available in the MCP Connectors tab.
AI Foundation is ready to explore
Return to AI Foundation to monitor usage or jump into AI Providers for deeper configuration.
Your configuration has been saved. Here’s a summary:
Visit AI Features to fine-tune each extension
Customise prompts in the AI Prompts library
